column1: #   factory   — Purplx permanent home. Flashed via serial (pio upload → 0x10000).
#               Cannot be overwritten by OTA — acts as an indestructible fallback.
#   ota_0     — Guest firmware slot. SDLauncher streams .bin files here.
#               Marked PENDING_VERIFY on boot; if guest never calls mark_valid()
#               the bootloader automatically rolls back to factory on next reset.
#   3. esp_restart() → bootloader sets ota_0 state to PENDING_VERIFY
#   4. Guest app runs. It does not know about Purplx
#   6. Bootloader sees ota_0 = PENDING_VERIFY (unconfirmed) → marks ABORTED
#   7. No valid OTA slot → bootloader falls back to factory partition (Purplx) ✓
# Purplx serial flash always writes to 0x10000 = factory start. ✓
# "pio run --target upload" naturally updates Purplx in factory. ✓
# Guest firmware cannot overwrite factory — safe by design. ✓
#   otadata   8KB    OTA slot selector (bootloader reads this)
#   factory   1.5MB  Purplx — ~220KB headroom from current ~1.28MB build
# IMPORTANT: partition layout changed — MUST erase chip before flashing:
#   pio run --target erase && pio run --target upload
# (subsequent uploads to factory do not need --target erase)
